title: acalypha novoguineensis
intro: Acalypha novoguineensis, also known as the New Guinea acalypha, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acalypha novoguineensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as aiding in wound healing and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by boiling its leaves in water, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or ointment for topical application.
